description: A narrative device — a rhetorical technique connecting beats to each other. Devices describe how the author arranges information for effect (foreshadowing, red herrings, setup/payoff, etc.). They reference beat IDs only — node and edge references live on the beats themselves.

type.description: Type of narrative device. FORESHADOWING — early hint at a later event. RED_HERRING — deliberate misdirection. SETUP_PAYOFF — something planted early that pays off later. DRAMATIC_IRONY — reader knows something the character does not. ECHO — a scene that mirrors an earlier one. CONTRAST — deliberate juxtaposition of two beats. PARALLEL — two storylines that run in parallel. CALLBACK — reference back to an earlier moment. CHEKHOV_GUN — element introduced early that must be used later. CUSTOM — any other narrative technique.

setup.description: Beat IDs where the device is planted

tags.description: Free-form tags for filtering and organization

examples.description: The journal found in the cave pays off when used to confront Elara.
Elara's mysterious behavior suggests danger but is actually protectiveness.
